easy.question: What is the small protruding metal cap on an electric cell called?
easy.explanation: The small protruding metal cap on one side of an electric cell is the positive terminal (+).

easy.question: When two or more cells are connected together, the combination is called a ______.
easy.explanation: A battery is formed when two or more cells are connected in series, with the positive terminal of one cell connected to the negative terminal of the next.

easy.question: In an incandescent lamp, which part glows to produce light?
easy.explanation: The filament is a thin wire inside the lamp that gets very hot and glows when electric current passes through it.

easy.question: In an LED, the longer wire is the ______ terminal.
easy.explanation: An LED has two wires -- the longer wire is the positive terminal (+) and the shorter wire is the negative terminal (-).

easy.question: How are cells connected in a battery?
options: Positive of one to negative of the next
easy.answer: Positive of one to negative of the next
easy.explanation: In a battery, cells are connected in series: the positive terminal of one cell is connected to the negative terminal of the next cell.

medium.question: How is an LED lamp different from an incandescent lamp? Name two differences.
medium.answer: An LED lamp has no filament, while an incandescent lamp has a thin filament that glows. An LED allows current to flow in only one direction and must be connected correctly (positive to positive), while an incandescent lamp works with current flowing in either direction. LEDs are also more energy efficient.
medium.explanation: LEDs and incandescent lamps work on different principles and have distinct properties.

medium.question: Why does connecting more cells together make a lamp glow brighter?
medium.answer: Connecting more cells together in a battery provides more electrical energy to the circuit. This means more current flows through the filament of the lamp, making it glow brighter. The battery also provides energy for a longer time.
medium.explanation: More cells in a battery increase the electrical energy available, resulting in brighter light.

hard.question: A student connects an LED to a battery but it does not glow. List all possible reasons why the LED might not glow and what the student should check.
hard.answer: The LED might not glow for several reasons: (1) The LED might be connected in the wrong direction -- the positive terminal of the LED (longer wire) must be connected to the positive terminal of the battery. LEDs only allow current in one direction. (2) The circuit might be incomplete -- there could be a loose connection or a broken wire. (3) The battery might be dead or weak -- the cells may have run out of chemical energy. (4) The LED itself might be damaged or burnt out. (5) The connecting wires might not have their plastic covering removed at the ends, preventing proper contact. The student should check the direction of the LED first, then verify all connections, and finally try a different battery or LED to isolate the problem.
hard.explanation: LEDs are directional devices and troubleshooting involves checking both the polarity and the completeness of the circuit.

easy.question: What provides a complete path for electric current to flow?
easy.explanation: An electrical circuit provides a complete path for electric current to flow from one terminal of the cell, through the components, and back to the other terminal.

easy.question: The direction of electric current is taken to be from the ______ terminal to the negative terminal of the cell.
easy.explanation: By convention, the direction of electric current in a circuit is from the positive terminal to the negative terminal of the electric cell.

easy.question: What happens when a switch is in the OFF position?
options: The circuit is closed and the lamp glows
The circuit is open and current stops flowing
easy.answer: The circuit is open and current stops flowing
easy.explanation: When the switch is OFF, it creates a gap in the circuit (open circuit), stopping the flow of current. The lamp does not glow.

easy.question: A drawing of a circuit using standard symbols is called a ______ diagram.
easy.explanation: A circuit diagram is a simplified drawing of an electrical circuit using standard symbols that are used worldwide.

medium.question: Explain the difference between an open circuit and a closed circuit.
medium.answer: In a closed circuit, the switch is ON and there is a complete path for current to flow. The lamp glows because current passes through it. In an open circuit, the switch is OFF and there is a gap that breaks the path. Current cannot flow through this gap, so the lamp does not glow.
medium.explanation: The state of the switch determines whether the circuit is open (OFF) or closed (ON).

medium.question: Why are standard symbols used in circuit diagrams instead of drawing actual pictures?
medium.answer: Standard symbols are used in circuit diagrams because drawing actual pictures of cells, lamps, and wires every time is difficult and time-consuming. Standard symbols make circuits easier to draw, read, and understand. Since these symbols are used worldwide, scientists and engineers from different countries can understand each other's circuits.
medium.explanation: Standard symbols provide a universal language for representing electrical circuits.

hard.question: Describe how you would make a simple switch using everyday materials. Explain the principle on which it works.
hard.answer: To make a simple switch, you need two drawing pins, a safety pin (or paper clip), a small piece of cardboard, and connecting wires. Steps: (1) Insert a drawing pin through the ring of the safety pin and fix it to the cardboard. (2) Fix the second drawing pin on the cardboard so that the free end of the safety pin can touch it. (3) Connect a wire to each drawing pin. The switch works on the principle that an electrical circuit needs a complete path for current to flow. When the safety pin touches both drawing pins (ON position), it creates a continuous conducting path (closed circuit), and current flows. When the safety pin is moved away from the second drawing pin (OFF position), there is a gap (open circuit), and current cannot flow. The switch can be placed anywhere in the circuit.
hard.explanation: A switch is simply a device that completes or breaks an electrical circuit by closing or opening a gap.

easy.question: Which of the following is a conductor of electricity?
easy.explanation: Copper is a metal and a good conductor of electricity. Most metals allow electric current to flow through them easily.

easy.question: Materials through which electric current cannot pass are called ______.
easy.explanation: Insulators (or poor conductors) are materials that do not allow electric current to pass through them, such as plastic, rubber, wood, and glass.

easy.question: Which metal is mainly used for making electric wires?
easy.explanation: Although silver is the best conductor, copper is mainly used for making wires because it is relatively cheaper and available in large quantities.

medium.question: Why are electric wires made of metal but covered with plastic?
medium.answer: Electric wires are made of metal (usually copper) because metals are good conductors of electricity and allow current to flow easily. They are covered with plastic because plastic is an insulator and does not allow current to flow through it. The plastic covering prevents electric shocks when someone touches the wire and also prevents short circuits.
medium.explanation: Both conductors and insulators are equally important -- conductors carry electricity while insulators keep us safe.

medium.question: What is the difference between Direct Current (DC) and Alternating Current (AC)?
medium.answer: Direct Current (DC) is produced by batteries and cells and flows in only one direction. It is used in small devices like torchlights, mobile phones, and remote controls. Alternating Current (AC) comes from power plants to our wall sockets and can run larger appliances like refrigerators, washing machines, and air conditioners.
medium.explanation: DC and AC are two types of electric current that differ in their direction of flow and applications.

hard.question: Our body is a conductor of electricity. Explain why this is dangerous and list at least four safety rules we should follow while dealing with electricity.
hard.answer: Since our body is a conductor of electricity, electric current can pass through our body, causing severe injury, burns, or even death. This makes it very important to follow safety rules: (1) Never touch switches or plugs with wet hands, as water makes our body an even better conductor. (2) Never use electrical devices in wet areas like bathrooms. (3) Never handle equipment with damaged insulation or broken plugs, as exposed wires can give electric shocks. (4) Never experiment with electricity from wall sockets or generators -- only use batteries for experiments. (5) Always ensure that wires are properly insulated and switches are in good condition. These precautions help prevent electric shocks and accidents.
hard.explanation: Understanding that our body conducts electricity is crucial for electrical safety.
